risk_on.description: Risk-on means the March 2026 stress baseline has materially eased: S&P 500 decisively above 6,506.48 (March 20 close), VIX back to low-teens from the March spike, and oil/rates no longer repricing inflation shock.
indicators: VIX < 18 and falling for at least 5 sessions
10-year Treasury yield stable or falling below 4.10%
WTI crude below 90 and no fresh supply-shock headlines
Breadth improves: >55% of S&P constituents above 20-day average
Growth sectors (technology/communication services) regain relative strength
rules: Favor momentum, trend, and volume breakout setups over pure mean reversion.
Increase position size only after two consecutive sessions of breadth confirmation.
Allow winners more room using trailing stops instead of fixed quick exits.
Prioritize long exposure in sectors showing both earnings revisions and price leadership.
Reduce index hedges gradually rather than all at once to avoid false dawn reversals.
Still cap single-position risk at 0.75R to respect residual geopolitical headline risk.

risk_off.description: Risk-off is the active seed regime on 2026-03-22: S&P 500 closed 6,506.48 and is down 5.0% YTD, VIX is around mid-20s after a 29.49 spike on March 6, Fed policy is 3.50-3.75%, and oil shock has pushed WTI near 98.32 and Brent 112.19.
indicators: S&P 500 below short-term trend and posting lower highs
10-year Treasury yield rising quickly (for example 4.25% to 4.38%)
WTI > 95 with persistent geopolitical risk premium
Fed cut expectations being repriced lower
Defensive/cyclical commodity sectors outperform growth beta
rules: Cut gross exposure and keep 30-40% cash available.
Reduce per-trade size to roughly half of normal and avoid oversized overnight risk.
Prefer relative-strength longs in energy/defensives and tactical mean reversion in oversold quality names.
Take profits faster (scale out at 1R) and avoid turning trades into investments.
Avoid first-breakout entries in weak breadth sessions; wait for retest confirmation.
Use hard stops and enforce a daily loss limit to prevent headline cascade losses.
If conflict headlines escalate late session, de-risk before the close rather than carry hope overnight.

neutral.description: Neutral is a transition state between the current risk-off backdrop and a full risk-on recovery: volatility cools but is not calm, cross-asset signals are mixed, and leadership rotates quickly.
indicators: S&P 500 oscillating around the 20-day moving average
Oil stabilizing roughly in the 85-95 zone
Sector leadership rotating week to week without broad confirmation
Fed communication interpreted as hold/wait rather than imminent easing
rules: Trade smaller and shorter: emphasize VWAP, support/resistance, and mean-reversion setups.
Take directional trades only near range extremes, not in the middle of the tape.
Favor liquid large caps where execution slippage is lower.
Demand multi-timeframe alignment before holding any swing position overnight.
Keep hedge overlays active until either breadth expansion or volatility compression confirms a new regime.
Review regime classification daily because neutral periods can flip quickly on one macro headline.
